notes: Closed 2018; course demolished. Formerly known as Apopka Golf & Tennis at Errol Estate.

description: Celebration Golf Club is the final collaborative design by Robert Trent Jones Sr. and Robert Trent Jones Jr., opened in 1996 as the centerpiece of Disney's master-planned Celebration community. The 18-hole layout winds through pine forests and Florida wetlands, rewarding accurate ball-strikers with a slope of 136 from the tips. Despite its upscale daily-fee positioning, the course remains open to the public without a membership requirement.

ChampionsGate Country Club is an 18-hole layout opened in 2015 within the ChampionsGate master-planned community southwest of Orlando. The course operates on a semi-private model, welcoming outside play while serving homeowner members. Its location 15 minutes from Disney World makes it a popular stop for resort-area golfers.

Highlands Reserve Golf Club is a Mike Dasher-designed 18-hole public course opened in 1998 in Davenport, set among the rolling hills of Polk County — terrain that provides elevation changes rare in Central Florida. The layout features generous fairways and well-bunkered greens suited for resort and vacation golfers visiting the nearby Disney corridor.

notes: Entry name '5 holes' is not a named golf course.
Entry name '16 holes' is not a named golf course.

description: Crane's Bend is a 9-hole, par-30 executive course at Orange Lake Resort (Holiday Inn Club Vacations) in Kissimmee, with each hole named after a bird species native to the area. Access is prioritized for resort owners and guests, though tee times are available to the public.
notes: Located at Orange Lake Resort, Kissimmee FL 34747. Resort/semi-private access.

description: Legends Walk at Orange Lake Resort is a 9-hole, par-27 walking course designed by Arnold Palmer, uniquely lit for night golf and certified for FootGolf. Located in the North Village of the resort, it offers a relaxed, family-friendly format with kids 12 and under playing free with a paying adult.
notes: Located at Orange Lake Resort, Kissimmee FL 34747. Par-3 walking course, lit for night golf.

description: Tranquilo Golf Club at Four Seasons Resort Orlando is a Tom Fazio-designed 18-hole course that originally opened as Osprey Ridge and was redesigned by Fazio in 2014. The par-71 layout stretches 6,901 yards through mature oaks, wetlands, and elevation changes, earning a place on Top 100 lists for Florida. Access is reserved for Four Seasons resort guests and Disney Golden Oak residents.
notes: Located at Four Seasons Resort Orlando at Walt Disney World, Golden Oak, FL 34747.

description: Heathrow Country Club is a Ron Garl-designed private 18-hole course opened in 1985 within the gated Heathrow community north of Orlando off I-4. The layout offers a classic Garl routing through stands of oaks and pine, with a slope rating that challenges single-digit handicappers.

The Ritz-Carlton Golf Club at Grande Lakes Orlando is an 18-hole Greg Norman design opened in 2003, routing 7,122 yards through live oaks, pines, and palmettos along the headwaters of the Florida Everglades. The course annually hosts the PNC Championship and is one of Central Florida's most prestigious resort layouts.
notes: Part of Grande Lakes Orlando resort, 4048 Central Florida Pkwy, Orlando FL 32837.

description: Remington Golf Club is an 18-hole public course opened in 1997 within the Remington planned community in Kissimmee, offering open Florida-style fairways at an accessible price point. The layout is well regarded for its conditioning and lack of housing along the fairways.

Kissimmee Bay Country Club is a Lloyd Clifton-designed semi-private course opened in 1990, routed through century-old oaks draped in Spanish moss and natural wetlands along Kissimmee Bay. The par-71 layout stretches 6,846 yards from the back tees and has earned a reputation as one of the most scenic settings in Osceola County.

Kissimmee Oaks Golf Club (now operating as Oaks National Golf Club) is a Gary Player and Karl Litten-designed 18-hole course opened in 1986, stretching 6,800-plus yards through mature oak corridors. The course was among the early Player Design projects in Central Florida and remains a popular daily-fee option.
notes: Now operating as Oaks National Golf Club, 1500 The Oaks Blvd, Kissimmee FL 34746.

description: The Cypress Course at Stonegate Golf Club is an 18-hole, par-72 layout designed by Clifton, Ezell & Clifton, opened in 2006 as part of the Solivita active-adult community in Poinciana. Measuring 7,100-plus yards, it pairs with the Oaks Course to form a 36-hole complex that welcomes outside play alongside community members.
notes: Physical address is Poinciana/Solivita (zip 34759), listed under Kissimmee.
Permanently closed. Course overgrown as of 2025.

description: Reunion Resort is the only destination in the world to feature signature championship courses by Arnold Palmer, Tom Watson, and Jack Nicklaus on the same property, all opened around 2004. The three 18-hole layouts each exceed 7,000 yards and offer distinct design philosophies on the same rolling terrain. Access is primarily for resort guests and homeowners, though outside tee times are bookable.
notes: Resort complex with three courses: Palmer, Watson, Nicklaus.

description: The Oaks Course at Stonegate Golf Club is an 18-hole, par-72 Ron Garl design opened in 2000 as the original course of the Solivita active-adult community in Poinciana. Stretching over 7,000 yards with a slope of 132 and a 73.1 USGA rating, it is one of the more demanding public-access layouts in the Kissimmee area.
notes: Physical address is Poinciana/Solivita (zip 34759), listed under Kissimmee.

description: Falcon's Fire Golf Club is an 18-hole Rees Jones-designed public course opened in 1993, consistently ranked among the best public courses in the Orlando metro area. Jones created a challenging but playable layout with water in play on most holes and a slope of 131. Its proximity to the US-192 tourism corridor makes it a frequent choice for visiting golfers.

Disney's Magnolia Golf Course is a Joe Lee-designed 18-hole layout that opened on October 1, 1971 — the same day as Magic Kingdom. Measuring 7,190 yards from the tips with a slope of 133, the Magnolia is the longest of Disney's courses and long served as the PGA Tour's Disney Classic venue. Access is reserved for Walt Disney World resort hotel guests.
notes: Disney World resort course; renovation announced 2026.

description: Disney's Lake Buena Vista Golf Course is a Joe Lee-designed 18-hole layout opened in 1972, winding through the Disney Springs and resort hotel corridor. The 6,802-yard par-72 course provides a tighter, tree-framed experience compared to Disney's other courses. Access is reserved for Walt Disney World resort guests.
notes: Closed May 2026 for major renovations; expected to reopen fall 2026.

description: Timacuan Golf Club (now marketed as Links of Lake Mary) is a Ron Garl-designed 18-hole semi-private course opened in 1987, later refined by Bobby Weed, with a slope of 137 ranking it among the more demanding layouts in Seminole County. Garl routed the course through natural Florida terrain with doglegs and elevation changes that reward course management.
notes: Also marketed as Links of Lake Mary. 550 Timacuan Blvd, Lake Mary FL 32746.

description: Alaqua Country Club is a Gary Player and Karl Litten-designed private 18-hole course opened in 1988 in the upscale Alaqua gated community, featuring elevated tees and bentgrass putting surfaces uncommon in Central Florida. The par-72 layout is a members-only, invitation-only club. Its setting amid the Wekiva River basin provides a forested, naturally quiet setting rare in the I-4 corridor.
notes: Strictly private, invitation only.

description: Wekiva Golf Club is an 18-hole semi-private course that has served the Longwood area since 1972, laid out across rolling terrain with no parallel fairways and large stands of oak framing every hole. The 6,850-yard par-72 layout rewards accurate driving and features multiple doglegs.

Forest Lake Golf Club is a public 18-hole championship course opened in 1994, set on 7,113 yards of parkland golf with no housing on the course — just mature trees, natural lakes, and open turf. The layout holds a slope of 128 and a 74.7 USGA rating and is regarded as an honest championship test at a daily-fee price.
notes: Address: 10521 Clarcona-Ocoee Rd, Apopka FL 34761.

description: The Legends at Orange Lake Resort is an 18-hole Arnold Palmer Signature course opened in 1998, stretching 7,072 yards through the hilly, rolling terrain of the Orange Lake Resort complex west of Kissimmee. Palmer routed the course with elevation changes up to 30 feet and strategic bunkering that rewards creative shotmaking.
notes: Located at Orange Lake Resort, 8505 W Irlo Bronson Memorial Hwy, Kissimmee FL 34747.

description: The Cypress Course at Grand Cypress Golf Club is a Jack Nicklaus-designed 18-hole layout opened in 2021 at the Evermore Resort in Orlando, drawing on natural wetlands and cypress corridors. The course is open to the public and serves as the flagship amenity of the Evermore Resort complex near Walt Disney World.
notes: Grand Cypress Golf Club at Evermore Resort, 1570 Evermore Way, Orlando FL 32836.

description: Shingle Creek Golf Club is a public 18-hole course opened in 2003, redesigned by the Arnold Palmer Design Company in 2016, adjacent to the Rosen Shingle Creek resort. The course sits at the headwaters of the Shingle Creek watershed and plays 7,228 yards from the tips as one of the longest and most demanding public tracks in Orlando.

MetroWest Golf Club is a Robert Trent Jones Sr.-designed 18-hole public course opened in 1987 in west Orlando. The par-72 course at 7,051 yards carries a slope of 128 and has built a strong reputation as a challenging but accessible daily-fee track. Its central location on Hiawassee Road makes it one of the most-played public courses in the Orlando metro.

Arnold Palmer's Bay Hill Club & Lodge is a private 18-hole course originally designed by Dick Wilson and opened in 1961, purchased by Arnold Palmer in the early 1970s and refined into one of the Tour's most demanding venues. The par-72 championship layout at 7,454 yards is home to the PGA Tour's Arnold Palmer Invitational.
notes: Private club with lodge; lodge guests may play.

description: Rio Pinar Country Club is a semi-private course with roots going back to 1957, one of the area's oldest surviving golf layouts. The 18-hole course hosted the PGA Tour's Florida Citrus Open multiple times in the 1970s. Today it operates as a semi-private club with membership options and public tee times.

The Country Club of Orlando is a members-only private club chartered in November 1911, making it the oldest golf and country club in Central Florida. The 18-hole layout at 1601 Country Club Drive has been refined over decades while retaining a traditional parkland character with mature trees and classic bunkering.
notes: Founded 1911; one of Florida's oldest country clubs.

description: Dubsdread Golf Course is a public 18-hole municipal course that opened on December 7, 1924 as the oldest public layout in Central Florida, owned by the City of Orlando since 1978 and fully renovated in 2008. The par-70 course at 549 W. Par Street plays 5,002 yards from the tips, offering a walkable, classic Florida layout with narrow tree-lined fairways that have challenged golfers for a century.
notes: Oldest public course in Central Florida; City of Orlando municipal course.

description: Orange Tree Golf Club is a private non-equity 18-hole course founded in 1972 within a gated residential community in the Bay Hill area of southwest Orlando. The course occupies rolling terrain adjacent to Turkey Lake and operates exclusively for members with no public access.
notes: Private non-equity; no public access.

description: Grande Vista Golf Club is a Ron Garl-designed 18-hole public course opened in 1997 as the centerpiece amenity of Marriott's Grande Vista resort complex in southeast Orlando, routed through the headwaters of the Florida Everglades ecosystem near SeaWorld. The par-71 layout rewards accuracy with tight fairways and numerous water features.
notes: Associated with Marriott's Grande Vista resort.

description: Waldorf Astoria Golf Club is an 18-hole Rees Jones-designed course opened in 2009 at the Waldorf Astoria Bonnet Creek resort, featuring majestic cypress trees lining fairways and a five-tee system accommodating all skill levels. Jones crafted a layout balancing resort playability with genuine challenge across 7,100 yards from the back tees.

Hunter's Creek Golf Club is a Lloyd Clifton-designed public 18-hole course opened in 1986, measuring 7,268 yards with a slope of 132 and a 75.1 USGA rating — one of the most demanding public layouts in the metro. Golf Digest has recognized it among the Top 75 Public Courses to Play in the country.

Disney's Palm Golf Course is a Joe Lee-designed 18-hole layout that opened on October 1, 1971 alongside Magic Kingdom. The par-72 course plays 6,870 yards from the championship tees with a slope of 131 and served as a longtime PGA Tour venue alongside the Magnolia course.
notes: Disney World resort course; access for resort guests.

description: Wedgefield Golf Club is a public 18-hole course opened in 1965 in the rural Wedgefield community of east Orange County, designed by Roy Albert Anderson. The layout at 20550 Maxim Pkwy stretches over 6,900 yards through open, wooded terrain far removed from the resort corridor.

Twin Rivers Golf Club is a Joe Lee-designed public 18-hole course opened in 1989, set between the Big and Little Econlockhatchee Rivers within a state wildlife preserve in Seminole County. The course rambles through natural Florida woodland with minimal development visible from the fairways, providing one of the most ecologically immersive rounds in the greater Orlando area.
notes: City of Oviedo municipal course.

description: Topgolf Orlando is a technology-enabled golf entertainment venue at 9295 Universal Blvd, offering climate-controlled hitting bays with ball-tracking shot analysis, a sports bar, restaurant, and event space. Interactive games and point-scoring targets replace a traditional fairway, making it accessible to all skill levels.
notes: Listed under Tangelo Park; physical address is Orlando FL 32819.

description: Orange County National Golf Center & Lodge is a 900-acre public golf resort featuring 36 holes designed by Phil Ritson, David Harman, and Isao Aoki, opened in 1997. The flagship Panther Lake course has served as a PGA Tour qualifying venue, and both courses are known for exceptional conditioning. Its lodge accommodations and practice complex make it a self-contained golf destination west of Orlando.

Winter Park Golf Course is a 9-hole, par-35 executive-length walking course built in 1914 — the second-oldest golf course in Central Florida. A major renovation in 2016 restored the course to excellent condition and made it one of the most celebrated short-course experiences in Florida.
notes: 9-hole public executive course; City of Winter Park municipal facility.

description: Tuscawilla Country Club is a Joe Lee-designed private 18-hole course opened in 1972 within the Tuscawilla gated community, featuring Diamond Zoysia greens and fairways framed by live oaks draped in Spanish moss. The club pairs its golf with Har-Tru clay tennis courts, a junior Olympic-size pool, and a restaurant.
